title: Physiotherapy Mobility Aid Prescription Cheat Sheet

prescription_criteria: Require extra point of contact with ground
Mild sensory/coordination problems
equipment_categories.image_prompt: Side view of an elderly person standing upright with a single-point walking stick held in one hand, demonstrating proper posture with elbow slightly flexed and stick positioned at wrist crease height when arm is relaxed at side

prescription_criteria: Patients with hemiplegia (requires physiotherapist consultation)
risks: Quad stick base can be a trip hazard
equipment_categories.image_prompt: Front and side view of a quad stick showing its four-pronged base on the floor, with measurement lines indicating proper height adjustment to user's wrist crease when standing with arm relaxed

prescription_criteria: Poor balance & reduced strength
Unable to control a 4-wheel frame
Non-weight bearing, partial weight bearing or amputee
Lacking coordination and stability to control 2-wheel frame (non-folding more stable)
risks: Fall risk if trying to pull up on frame when standing
Difficult to turn as front wheels are fixed
equipment_categories.image_prompt: Person demonstrating correct technique using a pick-up frame: standing within frame with both hands on grips, not leaning forward to pull up from sitting position, with frame positioned slightly ahead during ambulation

prescription_criteria: Poor balance & reduced strength
Unable to control a 4-wheel frame
Non-weight bearing, partial weight bearing or amputee
Lacking coordination and stability to control 2-wheel frame (non-folding more stable)
risks: Fall risk if trying to pull up on frame when standing
Difficult to turn as front wheels are fixed
equipment_categories.image_prompt: Close-up of 2-wheel frame showing fixed front skis and rear wheels, with arrows indicating proper lifting technique during gait cycle rather than dragging or pulling frame forward

equipment_categories.category_name: 4 Wheel Frame (4WF) with Hand Brakes - Standard
prescription_criteria: Cognitively able to utilise brakes
Adequate space around home to allow for mobility with frame
Stable enough to control 4WF but require two hand support to mobilise
Moderate to severe antalgic gait due to osteoarthritis (rigid frame also suitable)
risks: Fall risk if trying to pull up on frame when standing up
Brakes should be on prior to sitting down and before standing up to prevent movement of frame
Education required on safe positioning of frame prior to sitting down on frame
equipment_categories.image_prompt: Person demonstrating safe seated transfer technique with 4-wheel frame: brakes engaged, frame positioned at 45-degree angle to chair, user lowering self slowly while maintaining hand contact with frame handles

equipment_categories.category_name: 4 Wheel Frame (4WF) with Hand Brakes - Bariatric
prescription_criteria: Cognitively able to utilise brakes
Adequate space around home to allow for mobility with frame
Stable enough to control 4WF but require two hand support to mobilise
Moderate to severe antalgic gait due to osteoarthritis
risks: Fall risk if trying to pull up on frame when standing up
Brakes should be on prior to sitting down and before standing up to prevent movement of frame
Education required on safe positioning of frame prior to sitting down on frame
equipment_categories.image_prompt: Wider bariatric 4-wheel frame shown in home environment with adequate clearance around furniture, highlighting reinforced frame structure and wider seat base compared to standard models

models.name: Walking Tutor / Rear Wheels or Skis

prescription_criteria: Requiring more support
Need to offload a lower limb
Post orthopaedic surgery
Poor balance & coordination
Weak lower limbs but reasonable strength in upper limbs
risks: Fall risk if trying to pull up on frame when standing from bed/chair
equipment_categories.image_prompt: Person standing in forearm support frame with elbows resting in padded gutters at 90-degree flexion, shoulders relaxed, demonstrating proper weight distribution through forearms rather than hands

prescription_criteria: Requiring more support
Need to offload a lower limb
Post orthopaedic surgery
Poor balance & coordination
Weak lower limbs but reasonable strength in upper limbs
risks: Fall risk if trying to pull up on frame when standing from bed/chair
equipment_categories.image_prompt: Bariatric forearm support frame with extra-wide base and reinforced gutters, shown supporting a larger individual with proper elbow placement and upright posture

prescription_criteria: Cannot use SPS & require second point of contact with ground
Only have full use of one lower limb (e.g., amputation/injury)
Type of crutch based on comfort
risks: Pressure area risk under arm if leaning on crutches or if under arms when sitting down/standing up
equipment_categories.image_prompt: Person using Canadian crutches with hands gripping handgrips, elbows flexed at 20-30 degrees, crutch tips positioned slightly lateral and anterior to feet forming a stable tripod base of support

prescription_criteria: Cannot use SPS & require second point of contact with ground
Only have full use of one lower limb (e.g., amputation/injury)
Type of crutch based on comfort
risks: Pressure area risk under arm if leaning on crutches or if under arms when sitting down/standing up
equipment_categories.image_prompt: Side view showing correct axilla crutch fitting: two fingers' width (approximately 5cm) clearance between top of crutch pad and axilla, with hand gripping handgrip at wrist crease height

general_notes: Physiotherapist should always be consulted when prescribing equipment for any patient that is not cleared to weight bear as tolerated (WBAT)
Crutches in most cases not suitable for patients over 55 years of age

steps: Get person to stand upright with hands by side
Measure distance from floor to wrist crease
Add this information to referral form in brackets after equipment description, e.g., '4WF (handle height 80cm)'
handle_height_sps_4wf_2wf_puf.image_prompt: Person standing barefoot against wall with arms relaxed at sides, tape measure extending vertically from floor to natural wrist crease with palm facing body

crutch_measurements.hand_grip: Measured using same method as handle height (floor to wrist crease)
crutch_measurements.axilla_pad: Measured two fingers (or 5cm) below the axilla
crutch_measurements.image_prompt: Close-up diagram showing tape measure positioned 5cm below axilla for crutch pad height, with second measurement line from floor to wrist crease for handgrip height

steps: Person to stand in frame with arms in neutral position
Elbows at 90 degrees of flexion and shoulders relaxed
forearm_support_frame_gutters.image_prompt: Person standing within forearm support frame with elbows bent precisely at 90 degrees, forearms resting horizontally in padded gutters, shoulders relaxed and not hunched

specialised_equipment_note: Knee scooter, 1 handed walker, gutter crutches – require specialised physiotherapist assessment only
